options.a: going to an address in memory
options.b: locating an address with the help of another address
options.c: binding two addresses together to form a new address in a different memory space
options.d: a mapping from one address space to another

question: Binding of instructions and data to memory addresses can be done at ____________
If the process can be moved during its execution from one memory segment to another, then binding must be ____________

options.b: loading a routine only when it is called

question: What is the advantage of dynamic loading?

options.a: A used routine is used multiple times
options.b: An unused routine is never loaded

question: The idea of overlays is to ____________

options.a: data that are needed at any given time
options.b: enable a process to be larger than the amount of memory allocated to it
options.c: keep in memory only those instructions

question: The ___________ must design and program the overlay structure.
The ___________ swaps processes in and out of the memory.

If a higher priority process arrives and wants service, the memory manager can swap out the lower priority process to execute the higher priority process. When the higher priority process finishes, the lower priority process is swapped back in and continues execution. This variant of swapping is sometimes called?
If binding is done at assembly or load time, then the process _____ be moved to different locations after being swapped out and in again.
In a system that does not support swapping ____________

options.a: the compiler normally binds symbolic addresses (variables) to relocatable addresses
options.b: the compiler normally binds symbolic addresses to physical addresses
options.c: the loader binds relocatable addresses to physical addresses
options.d: binding of symbolic addresses to physical addresses normally takes place during execution

question: Which of the following is TRUE?

options.a: Overlays are used to increase the size of physical memory
options.b: Overlays are used to increase the logical address space
options.c: When overlays are used, the size of a process is not limited to the size of the physical memory
options.d: Overlays are used whenever the physical address space is smaller than the logical address space
